Title: Verify an audit record is created when a patient demographic record is created
Preconditions: Audit logging enabled; registration clerk logged in
Test_Steps: 1. Register a new synthetic patient | 2. Save the registration | 3. Open the audit/log viewer or query the audit store | 4. Locate the create event
Expected_Result: An audit entry exists for the create action capturing actor, action=CREATE, target patient UUID, UTC timestamp and source IP

Title: Verify update to PHI captures before and after values in the audit trail
Preconditions: Existing synthetic patient; auditing of changed fields enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Open an existing patient | 2. Edit the address field | 3. Save | 4. Inspect the audit entry for the update
Test_Data: Old Address=1 Old St; New Address=2 New Ave
Expected_Result: Audit entry records action=UPDATE with the prior and new field values (or a diff) for the changed attribute

Title: Verify voiding (soft delete) of a clinical observation is audited as a delete action
Preconditions: Patient with a saved observation; clinician logged in
Test_Steps: 1. Open the patient observation | 2. Void the observation with a reason | 3. Query the audit trail
Expected_Result: Audit entry records action=DELETE/VOID, the void reason, actor and timestamp; observation marked voided not physically removed

Title: Verify a read/view of a patient chart generates an access audit event
Preconditions: Access auditing enabled; clinician logged in
Test_Steps: 1. Search and open a patient dashboard | 2. View the clinical summary | 3. Query the access audit log
Expected_Result: An access (READ) audit event is recorded with actor, patient UUID, view context and timestamp

Title: Verify successful login is recorded with actor, timestamp and source
Preconditions: Audit logging enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate to login | 2. Authenticate with valid credentials | 3. Query the audit log for login events
Expected_Result: Audit entry shows event=LOGIN_SUCCESS with username, UTC timestamp, session id and originating IP

Title: Verify failed login attempts are audited with reason
Preconditions: Audit logging enabled; known user account exists
Test_Steps: 1. Submit login with a valid username and wrong password | 2. Repeat 3 times | 3. Query audit log
Expected_Result: Each failure records event=LOGIN_FAILURE with username, reason=bad credentials, timestamp and IP; no password value logged

Title: Verify logout and session timeout are distinguished in the audit trail
Preconditions: User logged in; short session timeout configured
Test_Steps: 1. Log in | 2. Log out explicitly and record event | 3. Log in again | 4. Idle until timeout | 5. Query audit log
Expected_Result: Audit distinguishes event=LOGOUT (user initiated) from event=SESSION_TIMEOUT with respective timestamps

Title: Verify account lockout after threshold failed logins is audited
Preconditions: Lockout threshold configured to N attempts
Test_Steps: 1. Submit N-1 bad logins (no lock) | 2. Submit Nth bad login | 3. Query audit log | 4. Attempt N+1
Expected_Result: At attempt N an event=ACCOUNT_LOCKED is audited; before N no lock event; attempt N+1 records blocked attempt

Title: Verify a data export of PHI generates an audit event with record count
Preconditions: Export feature available; user with export rights
Test_Steps: 1. Run a patient list or report export to CSV | 2. Confirm download | 3. Query audit log
Expected_Result: Audit entry records event=EXPORT, actor, dataset name, record/row count, destination format and timestamp

Title: Verify bulk FHIR Bulk Data export ($export) is logged with request parameters
Preconditions: FHIR Bulk Data endpoint available; OAuth client authorized
Test_Steps: 1. Invoke GET [base]/Patient/$export | 2. Poll the status URL | 3. Retrieve NDJSON | 4. Inspect server audit log
Expected_Result: AuditEvent records the $export request, requesting client, _type/_since params, status polling and the produced file set

Title: Verify a global property / configuration change is audited
Preconditions: Admin logged in; configuration auditing enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Open administration settings | 2. Change a global property value | 3. Save | 4. Query config audit log
Expected_Result: Audit entry records config change with property name, old value, new value, admin actor and timestamp

Title: Verify role/privilege assignment changes are audited
Preconditions: Admin logged in; RBAC auditing enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Open a user account | 2. Add a clinical role with PHI access | 3. Save | 4. Query the audit log
Expected_Result: Audit entry records the privilege grant: target user, role added, granting admin, timestamp; supports access-creep review

Title: Verify disabling audit logging is itself audited (anti-tamper control)
Preconditions: Admin with rights to toggle auditing
Test_Steps: 1. Open audit configuration | 2. Attempt to disable audit logging | 3. Save | 4. Re-enable and inspect log
Expected_Result: An audit event for the audit-config change is recorded before/at disable; the disable action is non-silent and reversible action also logged

Title: Verify audit records cannot be edited through the application UI
Preconditions: Audit entries exist; admin logged in
Test_Steps: 1. Open the audit log viewer | 2. Attempt to edit an existing audit entry | 3. Observe available controls
Expected_Result: No UI control permits editing an audit record; audit entries are read-only

Title: Verify direct UPDATE on the audit table is rejected or detectably violates integrity
Preconditions: Read access to audit store; test DB
Test_Steps: 1. Identify an audit row | 2. Attempt SQL UPDATE on its actor field | 3. Re-run tamper/integrity check
Expected_Result: UPDATE is denied by DB grants/triggers, or if applied the integrity verification flags the row as tampered

Title: Verify deletion of audit records is prevented or generates a higher-order audit
Preconditions: Audit rows exist; restricted DB account
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt to DELETE an audit row via UI and via SQL | 2. Observe result | 3. Inspect meta-audit
Expected_Result: Deletion is blocked; if a privileged purge runs, a meta-audit/retention event records who purged what and when

Title: Verify each audit record carries a cryptographic hash chained to the prior record
Preconditions: Tamper-evident audit configured (hash chaining)
Test_Steps: 1. Generate three sequential audit events | 2. Read stored hash of each | 3. Recompute hash(record + prev_hash) | 4. Compare
Expected_Result: Each record stores a hash equal to recomputed hash over its content plus previous record hash; chain verifies end to end

Title: Verify altering one audit record breaks the hash chain at and after that record
Preconditions: Hash-chained audit with at least 5 records
Test_Steps: 1. Capture chain valid baseline | 2. Mutate the content of record 3 in store | 3. Run chain verification
Expected_Result: Verification fails starting at record 3; records 1-2 still verify; report pinpoints the first broken link

Title: Verify periodic audit integrity job alerts on chain or checksum mismatch
Preconditions: Scheduled integrity verification job configured; alerting set
Test_Steps: 1. Introduce a checksum mismatch | 2. Trigger the integrity job | 3. Inspect alert channel
Expected_Result: Job detects mismatch and raises an alert/notification to the compliance team with affected record range

Title: Verify audit records are retained for the configured minimum period
Preconditions: Retention policy configured (e.g., 6 years); date manipulation possible in test
Test_Steps: 1. Create an audit record dated just under retention age | 2. Run retention/purge job | 3. Verify record still present
Expected_Result: Record younger than retention boundary is retained after the purge job runs

Title: Verify audit records older than retention plus grace are purged with a purge audit entry
Preconditions: Retention configured; purge job available
Test_Steps: 1. Seed a record dated just beyond retention+grace | 2. Run purge job | 3. Verify removal and meta-audit
Expected_Result: Over-age record is purged; a purge/meta-audit entry records the purge action, count and operator

Title: Verify legal hold suspends purge of audit records under hold
Preconditions: Legal hold feature available; retention purge job
Test_Steps: 1. Place a legal hold on a patient/audit subset | 2. Age records beyond retention | 3. Run purge | 4. Vary hold on/off and over/under age
Expected_Result: Records under active legal hold are never purged regardless of age; only non-held over-age records are purged

Title: Verify who-accessed-this-patient report lists all accessors with timestamps
Preconditions: Multiple users have viewed a target patient; access auditing on
Test_Steps: 1. Open compliance access report | 2. Filter by target patient | 3. Run report
Expected_Result: Report lists every distinct accessor, access action, context and UTC timestamp for the patient; matches recorded access events

Title: Verify what-did-user-access report (who/what/when/where) for a single actor
Preconditions: Target user has performed multiple PHI actions
Test_Steps: 1. Open user activity report | 2. Filter by actor and date range | 3. Run
Expected_Result: Report shows what records, what actions, when (timestamp) and where (workstation/IP) for the actor; covers who/what/when/where

Title: Verify access report includes the where dimension (IP/workstation/location)
Preconditions: Access events capture source location
Test_Steps: 1. Generate access from two distinct IPs | 2. Run access report | 3. Inspect where column
Expected_Result: Report distinguishes access by source IP/workstation/location for each event

Title: Verify access report pagination and totals are correct for high-volume actor
Preconditions: Actor with >1000 access events
Test_Steps: 1. Run access report for the high-volume actor | 2. Page through results | 3. Compare displayed total to source count
Expected_Result: Pagination renders all pages, no duplicates/gaps, displayed total equals source event count

Title: Verify minimum-necessary review flags a user accessing patients outside their care team
Preconditions: Care-team relationships defined; outlier detection report
Test_Steps: 1. Have a user view patients not on their assigned panel | 2. Run minimum-necessary/outlier report
Expected_Result: Report flags out-of-panel accesses as potential minimum-necessary violations for compliance review

Title: Verify emergency break-glass access requires a justification reason before granting
Preconditions: Break-glass workflow configured; restricted record exists
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt to open a restricted/VIP patient | 2. Trigger break-glass prompt | 3. Enter justification | 4. Proceed
Expected_Result: Access is granted only after a justification is captured; reason stored with the break-glass event

Title: Verify break-glass access is logged with elevated severity and reason
Preconditions: Break-glass performed in prior step
Test_Steps: 1. Perform break-glass access | 2. Query audit log filtered to break-glass
Expected_Result: Audit entry marks event as break-glass/emergency override, includes justification, actor, patient and timestamp, flagged high severity

Title: Verify break-glass without justification is denied and the denied attempt is audited
Preconditions: Break-glass workflow enforces mandatory reason
Test_Steps: 1. Trigger break-glass | 2. Leave justification blank | 3. Submit
Expected_Result: Access denied; a denied break-glass attempt is audited; no PHI is disclosed

Title: Verify break-glass events trigger post-hoc compliance review notification
Preconditions: Break-glass logged; review workflow configured
Test_Steps: 1. Perform break-glass access | 2. Observe review task creation | 3. Reviewer marks reviewed | 4. Check status
Expected_Result: Break-glass event moves Open -> Under Review -> Closed with reviewer and disposition recorded

Title: Verify mass-record access in a short window raises an anomaly alert
Preconditions: Anomaly detection thresholds configured
Test_Steps: 1. Have one user open 50 distinct charts in 5 minutes | 2. Let detection job run | 3. Inspect alerts
Expected_Result: An anomaly/breach alert is raised for unusual volume with actor, count and window

Title: Verify same-last-name access (snooping on relatives/self) is flagged
Preconditions: Employee directory linked; snooping rule enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Have a user access a patient sharing their surname | 2. Run snooping detection
Expected_Result: Access to same-surname or self record is flagged for review as potential snooping

Title: Verify off-hours access generates a low-severity advisory event
Preconditions: Business-hours window configured for detection
Test_Steps: 1. Generate access at 02:00 local | 2. Generate access at 14:00 | 3. Run detection
Expected_Result: Off-hours (02:00) access is flagged as advisory; in-hours (14:00) is not flagged

Title: Verify breach detection thresholds across record-count and time-window pairs
Preconditions: Detection rule with count and window parameters
Test_Steps: 1. Run combinations of access count and time window | 2. Observe alert vs no-alert per pair
Expected_Result: Alerts fire only for pairs exceeding the configured rate threshold; below-threshold pairs do not alert, per the pairwise matrix

Title: Verify a PHI read maps to a conformant FHIR AuditEvent resource
Preconditions: FHIR AuditEvent endpoint available; OAuth token with scope
Test_Steps: 1. Perform a patient read | 2. GET [base]/AuditEvent?patient=... | 3. Validate the resource
Expected_Result: Returned AuditEvent has type/subtype (rest/read), recorded timestamp, agent (who), source (where) and entity (what) per FHIR R4

Title: Verify AuditEvent.outcome reflects a failed/denied access (4-8 codes)
Preconditions: FHIR API enforces authorization
Test_Steps: 1. Issue a read without sufficient scope | 2. Retrieve the corresponding AuditEvent
Expected_Result: AuditEvent.outcome is non-zero (minor/serious failure) with outcomeDesc indicating authorization denial

Title: Verify AuditEvent.agent.requestor and network address are populated
Preconditions: FHIR AuditEvent generation enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Perform an action via FHIR client | 2. Retrieve AuditEvent | 3. Inspect agent and network elements
Expected_Result: agent.requestor=true for the human/client initiating, agent.network.address holds source IP/identifier

Title: Verify an ATNA/RFC3881 syslog audit message is emitted for a query transaction
Preconditions: ATNA audit record repository configured; secure syslog
Test_Steps: 1. Trigger a PIX/PDQ or HL7 query | 2. Capture the audit message at the repository
Expected_Result: An IHE ATNA audit message (DICOM/RFC3881 schema) is received with EventID, ActiveParticipant and ParticipantObject for the query

Title: Verify ADT message processing creates an audit entry linking message control id to action
Preconditions: HL7 ADT interface live; auditing enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Send an ADT^A08 update | 2. Process the message | 3. Query the audit log
Expected_Result: Audit entry references the HL7 message control id (MSH-10), sending app and the resulting patient update action

Title: Verify each audited event type maps to a HIPAA audit-control requirement
Preconditions: Regulatory mapping document/matrix available
Test_Steps: 1. Enumerate audited event types | 2. Cross-reference each to HIPAA 164.312(b) audit controls | 3. Identify gaps
Expected_Result: Every required event (access, create, update, delete, export, auth) maps to a regulatory control; gaps are reported as findings

Title: Verify audit fields satisfy the minimum dataset (who/what/when/where/outcome)
Preconditions: Audit schema documented
Test_Steps: 1. For each event category list captured fields | 2. Check presence of actor, action, target, timestamp, source, outcome | 3. Score
Expected_Result: All event categories contain the minimum required fields; any missing field is reported as a compliance gap

Title: Verify GDPR right-of-access request can be fulfilled from audit data within SLA
Preconditions: Subject access request workflow; audit query tooling
Test_Steps: 1. Receive a synthetic subject access request | 2. Compile all access/processing records for the subject | 3. Package report
Expected_Result: A complete access/processing report for the data subject is produced and verifiable within the defined SLA

Title: Verify all audit timestamps are stored in UTC and rendered with timezone
Preconditions: Server and client in different timezones
Test_Steps: 1. Generate an event from a client in a non-UTC zone | 2. Inspect stored timestamp | 3. View in report
Expected_Result: Stored timestamp is UTC; report displays with explicit timezone offset; no ambiguous local-only time

Title: Verify audit ordering is preserved across a daylight-saving transition
Preconditions: Test clock can cross a DST boundary
Test_Steps: 1. Generate events spanning the DST fall-back hour | 2. Sort audit by timestamp
Expected_Result: Events retain monotonic ordering (UTC based); no duplicate-hour reordering or out-of-sequence records

Title: Verify only compliance/admin roles can view the audit log
Preconditions: Roles configured; non-privileged user available
Test_Steps: 1. Log in as a regular clinician | 2. Attempt to open the audit log viewer | 3. Log in as compliance officer and retry
Expected_Result: Clinician is denied access to audit logs; compliance role can view; the denied attempt is itself audited

Title: Verify audit log viewer enforces field-level masking of sensitive payloads
Preconditions: Audit entries may contain PHI in before/after values
Test_Steps: 1. Open an audit entry containing PHI diff as a viewer without unmask rights | 2. Inspect displayed payload
Expected_Result: Sensitive payload values are masked for users lacking unmask privilege; full values only for authorized roles

Title: Verify audit query API enforces pagination limit and rejects oversize page requests
Preconditions: Audit query API exposed; auth token
Test_Steps: 1. GET audit API with _count above max | 2. GET with valid _count | 3. Inspect responses
Expected_Result: Oversize request is capped or rejected with 400; valid request returns paged results with next-page link

Title: Verify audit query API requires authentication and authorization
Preconditions: Audit API endpoint deployed
Test_Steps: 1. Call audit API with no token | 2. Call with token lacking audit scope | 3. Call with proper scope
Test_Data: No token / wrong scope / right scope
Expected_Result: Unauthenticated returns 401; under-privileged returns 403; authorized returns 200; all attempts audited

Title: Verify exported audit report is digitally signed or checksummed for non-repudiation
Preconditions: Audit export feature with signing/checksum
Test_Steps: 1. Export an audit report | 2. Capture the signature/checksum | 3. Tamper one byte | 4. Re-verify
Expected_Result: Original verifies against signature/checksum; tampered file fails verification, proving integrity protection

Title: Verify the system fails safe when the audit store is unavailable
Preconditions: Ability to take the audit store offline in test
Test_Steps: 1. Take the audit store offline | 2. Attempt a PHI write action | 3. Observe behavior | 4. Restore store
Expected_Result: Per policy the PHI action is blocked or queued (no silent unaudited write); a system alert is raised; queued events flush on restore

Title: Verify buffered audit events are not lost on application crash
Preconditions: Audit buffering/queue enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Generate audit events | 2. Kill the app process before flush | 3. Restart | 4. Verify events present
Expected_Result: No buffered audit events are lost; on restart all events are persisted exactly once (no loss, no duplication)

Title: Verify recording and revoking patient consent generates audit events
Preconditions: Consent management available
Test_Steps: 1. Record a consent | 2. Verify audit | 3. Revoke the consent | 4. Verify audit
Expected_Result: Both grant and revoke transitions are audited with actor, consent scope, effective time and resulting state

Title: Verify access blocked by consent restriction is audited as a denied access
Preconditions: Consent restricts a record; requesting user lacks override
Test_Steps: 1. Apply a deny consent on a record | 2. Attempt access as restricted user | 3. Query audit
Expected_Result: Access is denied and a denied-access audit event is recorded citing the consent policy as the reason

Title: Verify audit log viewer is keyboard navigable and screen-reader labeled
Preconditions: Audit viewer UI; screen reader available
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate the audit viewer using only keyboard | 2. Tab through filters and table | 3. Inspect ARIA labels with a screen reader
Expected_Result: All controls reachable and operable by keyboard; table rows and filters have accessible names/roles; focus order is logical

Title: Verify audit search filters (actor, patient, date range, event type) return correct subsets
Preconditions: Audit data spanning multiple actors and event types
Test_Steps: 1. Filter by actor only | 2. Add date range | 3. Add event type | 4. Compare counts to source
Expected_Result: Each added filter narrows results to the correct subset; combined filters apply as AND; counts match source query

Title: Verify printing or generating a patient document is audited as a disclosure
Preconditions: Document/print feature; disclosure auditing enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Open a patient summary | 2. Generate/print a PDF or document | 3. Query audit
Expected_Result: A disclosure/print audit event is recorded with actor, patient, document type and timestamp

Title: Verify a patient record merge audits both source and target identifiers
Preconditions: Two duplicate synthetic patients; merge privilege
Test_Steps: 1. Initiate patient merge | 2. Select surviving record | 3. Complete merge | 4. Query audit
Expected_Result: Audit records the merge with both source and surviving patient identifiers, actor, timestamp and reason
